snapshot.source_name: Regional District of Central Kootenay Active Applications
snapshot.permit_type: The subject property is located on the east side of Kootenay Lake in Murphy Creek. It is boat access only, with no road connecting the neighboring Murphy Creek community. The Property is 54 acres in size, has a western aspect, and is bordered by the Purcell Wilderness Conservancy Park to the north, Crown land to the east and Clute Creek to the south. This Watercourse Development Permit (WDP) application has been submitted in response to the property owner’s unauthorized disturbance and construction of various buildings within the 30m watercourse development permit area (WDPA).

snapshot.source_name: Regional District of Central Kootenay Active Applications
snapshot.permit_type: The owners are proposing to build an accessory dwelling unit on the undeveloped “west portion” property near Loff Road and are requesting a variance to the maximum permitted size of a Farm Residential Footprint to do so. The map and site plan included in this package show the area of existing development (where there are no changes proposed) and the area of proposed development, where the new residence is proposed to be sited. The accessory dwelling unit would be serviced by its own well and septic system and would occupy an approximate area of 2,000 square metres.

snapshot.source_name: Regional District of Central Kootenay Active Applications
snapshot.permit_type: The purpose of this Site-Specific Floodplain Exemption (SSFE) application is to authorize the construction of an accessory building (bunkhouse) at 7m from the natural boundary of Crystal Creek whereas the RDCK Floodplain Management Bylaw requires a building setback distance of 15m from the natural boundary of Crystal Creek. Thus the total proposed setback reduction requested in 8m.

snapshot.source_name: Regional District of Central Kootenay Active Applications
snapshot.permit_type: The subject property is in Six Mile and is bisected by Hamilton Road and Highway 3A. The property is currently vacant. The applicant has submitted a Development Variance Permit (DVP) in order to allow the construction of a residence which has eaves that project into the side yard setbacks. To achieve this, the applicant is requesting to vary the zoning bylaw as follows: Section 606(4) to allow the eaves to project 1.0m into the side setback – an increase of 0.4m. If approved, this variance would authorize the eaves to extend to 1.5m from the side lot line.

snapshot.source_name: Regional District of Central Kootenay Active Applications
snapshot.permit_type: The subject parcel is partially within the Agricultural Land Reserve (ALR). The applicant has submitted a Development Variance Permit application in order to permit an oversized accessory dwelling unit (ADU) on the property and to site a residence near the rear of the lot. To achieve this, the applicant is requesting to vary the zoning bylaw as follows: Section 623(4) to allow a 155m2 ADU – an increase of 65m2, Section 2701(3) to allow a 12,000m2 farm residential footprint area – an increase of 9500m2 and Section 2701(4) to allow a 646m farm residential footprint depth – an increase of 586m. The proposed oversized ADU is to be sited on the portion of the property not within the ALR.

snapshot.source_name: Regional District of Central Kootenay Active Applications
snapshot.permit_type: In January and February 2026, the Rural Affairs Committee directed staff to prepare amendments to revise how density is calculated in most residential zones within Areas A, B, C, and D, as well as one zone in Area I. The intent is to return to the previous density calculation method used before the adoption of Bylaws 2953, 2954, 2955, 2956, and 2958 in 2024. The purpose of these bylaw amendments is: 1. Density Calculation: The proposal would change how residential density is calculated in most R1, R2, R3, and R4 zones from a “lot area” basis to a “site area” basis. This does not increase the total density allowed on a property, but it does change how that density can be achieved. Under the current zoning, a subdivision is typically required to reach maximum density, whereas under the proposed approach, the same density could be achieved without subdividing the land. 2. Housekeeping: Clarify the bylaws through formatting and terminology updates.

snapshot.source_name: Regional District of Central Kootenay Active Applications
snapshot.permit_type: The 2.38 ha subject property is located in Thrums in Electoral Area ‘I’ and has been developed with two single detached homes as shown on the attached site plan. The purpose of this bylaw amendment is to consider allowing three single detached homes on the residential lot. The applicant is also proposing to increase the minimum lot size for subdivision to 2.0 hectares to prevent additional residential development on the lot through subdivision. To achieve this, the applicant is proposing to rezone from Country Residential I (CRI) to Country Residential I (CRI) Site-Specific.

snapshot.description:
Low Density Housing - New Building - To construct a 2 storey  two-family dwelling ($710,097.50) with a detached accessory building (garage) and 1 open parking at the rear providing 2 parking spaces, having vehicular access from the lane.

1.\tNo A/C Unit proposed
2.\tSchedule B: (Jason Hui) P.Eng (778.319.3403) Structural & Geotechnical
3.\tHPO: Residential Builder- JHN Development Ltd.

Note: This is a 2 storey building pursuant to the Zoning and Development By-Law

Address Information:

Principle Dwelling #1: 1820 E 12 Ave
Principle Dwelling #2: 1818 E 12 Ave

Addresses have been assigned per the approved plans, access to the rear unit is from the North side. This access must be provided & maintained at all times and the building addresses posted to be visible from the street in accordance with the Building By-law.

**THIS PERMIT HAS BEEN ISSUED UNDER THE REQUIREMENTS OF VBBL #12511 (2019)**

This Building Permit will remain in a suspended status until the associated (green) Demolition Permit BP-2020-02365 is completed

****ALL PROJECT COORIDINATOR NOTES HAVE BEEN ACCEPTED BY THE APPLICANT ****

snapshot.permit_family: Building and construction
snapshot.description:
Low Density Housing - New Building - To construct a 2 storey laneway house building ($214,822.5) with 1 open parking pad, providing a total of 1 parking spaces, having vehicular access from the 20' Lane.

Note:  This is a 1 ½ storey building pursuant to the Zoning & Development By-law.

1.\tCovenant not required with existing house
2.\tNo Strata Titling permitted
3.\tNo A/C Unit proposed
4.\tEntire building to be sprinklered 13D 
5.\tSchedule B: (Zhanchao Zhao) P.Eng (604.526.1305) Structural
6.\tSchedule B: (Zhanchao Zhao) P.Eng (604.526.1305) Geotechnical
7.\tHPO: Residential Builder- AR Event Solutions Inc.

NOTE: To achieve the required FSR. A floor space exclusion of 3% has been applied to this application as per section 11.24.18 of the district schedule, to accommodate improved building performance.

Address Information:

Principle Dwelling: #1-5559 Earles St
Laneway home: #3-5559 Earles St

Address and suite number assigned as per approved plans for Fire and Emergency response. The address number is to be posted to the building to be visible from the street and the suite number is to be posted at the suite entry in accordance with the Building By-law prior to final inspection.

NOTE: Information regarding the principal building on the survey and site plan is for reference only. Any discrepancy between information regarding the principal building on the survey and/or site plan and approved permit drawings for the principal building are neither approved nor authorized under this laneway house permit.

*EXISTING HOUSE* Note: Bldg 1 (principal bldg) addressed #1-5559 Earles St retained on site.

***This permit is issued under the VBBL 2019, with updated energy requirements as per bylaw # 12997 effective Jan 01, 2022.***.

snapshot.permit_family: Building and construction
snapshot.description:
Low Density Housing - New Building - To construct a 2 storey + basement one-family dwelling with a secondary suite located in the basement with an open parking pad, at the rear providing 1 parking spaces, having vehicular access from the lane.

Note: This is a 2 storey building pursuant to the Zoning and Development By-Law
                                                              
1- Covenant registered at the Land Title Office under CA9942391.
2- No strata titling permitted.
3- Bar sink proposed in main floor.
4- A/C unit proposed in rear yard.
5- Schedule B:  Jason Hui, P.Eng (778.319.3403) Structural & Geotechnical
6- HPO: Royalsun Enterprises Ltd.

Building 1
#1-3339 Seaforth Drive– One Family Dwelling (East side basement, 1st & 2nd storey)
#2-3339 Seaforth Drive = Sec Suite (West side basement)

Address and suite numbers assigned as per approved plans for Fire and Emergency response. The address number is to be posted to the building to be visible from the street and the suite numbers are to be posted at the suite entries in accordance with the Building By-law prior to final inspection. 

Combustible projections or roof soffits on an exposing building face shall not project to less than .45m from the property line and shall be in compliance with VBBL 2019 9.10.15.5.(9)                                                         
                                                                                 
The entire building, to be sprinklered to NFPA 13D.

******THIS PERMIT HAS BEEN ISSUED UNDER THE REQUIREMENTS OF VBBL #12511 (2019)******

snapshot.source_name: City of Vancouver Issued Building Permits Open Data
snapshot.permit_family: Building and construction
snapshot.description:
Low Density Housing - New Building - To construct a 2 storey laneway house building with an attached garage and an open parking pad providing 2 parking spaces having vehicular access from the lane.

The entire building, INCLUDING THE ATTACHED GARAGE, to be sprinklered to NFPA 13R.

NOTE: To achieve the required FSR a floor space exclusion of 3% has been applied to this application as per section 11.17.24(d) of the district schedule, to accommodate improved building performance.

1.\tCovenant registered at the Land Title Office under CA9763203
2.\tNo Strata Titling permitted
3.\tA/C unit proposed in rear yard
4.\tSchedule B Jason Hui P.Eng (778-319-3403) Structural and Geotechnical
5.\tBC Housing Cypher Developments Ltd.

Note: This is a 2 storey building pursuant to the Zoning and Development By-Law                                                                            
******THIS PERMIT HAS BEEN ISSUED UNDER THE REQUIREMENTS OF VBBL 2019******

"Combustible projections or roof soffits on an exposing building face shall not project to less than .45m from the property line and shall be in compliance with VBBL 2019 9.10.15.5.(9)"
 
Any alterations or conversions to the attached garage (if applicable) to habitable living space shall not be permitted without first obtaining the approval of the Director of Planning and the Chief Building Official. Thermal protection measures and Laneway house design guidelines shall be required to be met prior to the approval of the conversion.

See DB-2021-06560 for building on site (principal building) addressed 2436 E 48th Ave

Assigned Addressing:

Building 2
#3-2436 E 48th Av - Laneway House (1.5 storey)

snapshot.description:
Low Density Housing - New Building - To construct a 2 storey laneway house building with an open parking pad, providing 1 parking space, having vehicular access from the lane.

Note:  This is a 1 ½ storey building pursuant to the Zoning & Development By-law

1.\tNo A/C unit proposed
2.\tSchedule B:  Zhanchao Zhao (604-526-1305) Structural & Geotechnical
3.\tHPO: AR Event Solutions Inc.

Building 2
#3-2812 W 13th Av - Laneway House

Note
As a result of the addition of a laneway house, the existing principal dwelling / single detached house with a secondary suite, are to be assigned suite numbers. At permit issuance, the site will be as follows:
#1-2812 W 13th Av - Principal Dwelling
#2-2812 W 13th Av - Secondary Suite 
#3-2812 W 13th Av - Laneway House 

Address and suite numbers assigned as per approved plans for Fire and Emergency response. The address number is to be posted to the building to be visible from the street and the suite numbers are to be posted at the suite entries in accordance with the Building By-law prior to final inspection.

This permit is issued under the VBBL 2019, with updated energy requirements as per bylaw # 12997 effective Jan 01, 2022.

Information regarding the principal building on the survey and site plan is for reference only. Any discrepancy between information regarding the principal building on the survey and/or site plan and approved permit drawings for the principal building are neither approved nor authorized under this laneway house permit.                                                     
                                                                                                                               
The entire building, to be sprinklered to NFPA 13D.
                                                                              
THIS PERMIT HAS BEEN ISSUED UNDER THE REQUIREMENTS OF VBBL #12511 (2019)

snapshot.permit_family: Development and planning
snapshot.description: The City is considering a Delegated Heritage Alteration Permit application for the installation of 5 skylights and the replacement of a wood fire exit door on the east side with a similar door with required hardware. The additions of the skylights on the north rear side and within an east roof valley are considered modifications to the roof that do not negatively affect the building's heritage value, and the integrity of the historic place is not impaired by the new work if it was removed in the future. The installation and location of the skylights are therefore consistent with the Standards and Guidelines for the Conservation of Historic Places in Canada.
